title: Matthew McConaughey | Career Arc
format: Sean Fennessey, Amanda Dobbins, and Rob Harvilla trace the McConaissance: each host's breakthrough, each host's personal pinnacle, the rom-com decade and recent bricks in between, and The Beach Bum as the role truest to him.

films.title: Dazed and Confused
films.note: Rob's pick. Wooderson, on screen maybe ten minutes: 'I get older, they stay the same age.' Instant stardom, and a charming predator the movie can't bear to make pathetic.

films.title: A Time to Kill
films.note: Sean's pick. The first time he's in the center of the frame the whole time, playing modern-day Atticus Finch in a ludicrous Grisham thriller he single-handedly holds up. The five-minute 'now imagine she's white' closing.

films.title: Magic Mike
films.note: Amanda's pick. Fully greased up and leaning into the voice, a supporting mentor role where the bongo guy finally clicks into the public consciousness and the McConaissance takes off.

films.title: Texas Chainsaw Massacre: The Next Generation
films.note: Quite bad, per Sean.

films.title: Boys on the Side
films.note: A small, very charming role.

films.title: Lone Star
films.note: A smaller but crucial part.

films.title: Amistad
films.note: Back to the courtroom a year later, another racially complicated only-in-the-'90s movie.

segments.heading: Personal pinnacles
segments.note: each host's peak McConaughey (True Detective, Rob's actual pick, is TV)

films.title: Killer Joe
films.note: Sean's pinnacle. A purposefully evil Friedkin/Tracy Letts Texas tale of murder and incest; not his most rewatchable, but the choice that signals him taking chances again.

films.title: How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days
films.note: Amanda's pinnacle. The most successful of the rom-coms and a real reference point for the genre after it, even if it's a sour, eggshell-walking rewatch. Rob saw it five days ago.

films.title: The Lincoln Lawyer
films.note: The start of the turn in 2011 back toward interesting filmmakers; airport-fiction legal thriller, and exactly his soliloquizer register.

films.title: The Paperboy
films.note: A ludicrous, everyone-going-for-it totem of a broken society, per Sean.

films.title: Tropic Thunder
films.note: One of his best minor-key comic roles, as the agent, from the fallow period.

films.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
films.note: Mark Hanna, the chest-beating 'fugazi' speech that sets the agenda for DiCaprio's Belfort.

films.title: Dallas Buyers Club
films.note: The Oscar. None of the three love it; Rob calls it the quintessential I-want-an-Oscar movie, Green Book by other means, a year after Magic Mike.

segments.heading: The rom-com decade and the recent bricks
segments.note: the fallow stretch, then the good-on-paper misses

films.title: The Wedding Planner
films.note: Beloved by a certain segment; J.Lo and McConaughey both doing things far from what they do now.

films.title: Sahara
films.note: Where, with Two for the Money, things start to go south.

films.title: Failure to Launch
films.note: Amanda's example of the tough 2005-2009 leading-man rom-coms, with Fool's Gold and Ghosts of Girlfriends Past.

films.title: Fool's Gold
films.note: A tough 2005-2009 leading-man rom-com, not good.

films.title: Interstellar
films.note: A big Nolan hit Sean isn't a fan of.

films.title: The Sea of Trees
films.note: A Gus Van Sant debacle set in Japan's suicide forest, later infamous from a Logan Paul video.

films.title: Free State of Jones
films.note: A forgotten, similarly white-savior movie.

films.title: Gold
films.note: Sean found it kind of interesting; it came and went.

films.title: The Dark Tower
films.note: The long-awaited Stephen King adaptation. Terrible.

films.title: Serenity
films.note: An all-time January curio with Anne Hathaway.

segments.heading: The Beach Bum
segments.note: Harmony Korine, the role truest to him

films.title: The Beach Bum
films.note: A leisurely 90-minute movie about a burnout poet married to a wealthy Miami woman: aspirational hedonism, Wooderson taken to his logical conclusion. Snoop Dogg, Isla Fisher, Zac Efron, Jimmy Buffett, Martin Lawrence, and a scene-stealing Jonah Hill. Rob saw it alone at 9:50 a.m.

Texas Chainsaw Massacre: The Next Genera.title: The Return of the Texas Chainsaw Massacre

How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days.title: How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days
